title: Dense Ethiopian Paneer Wot
description: A rich and flavorful Ethiopian dish featuring paneer, served over a bed of injera bread. Perfect for a date night or special occasion.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the onion, garlic, and ginger to the pan and saut 9 until the onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the chili peppers to the pan and cook for another minute.
Add the cumin, coriander, turmeric, and shiro wot spice blend to the pan and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the paneer to the pan and cook until it is browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the water to the pan and bring to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, until the sauce has thickened and the paneer is coated, about 20 minutes.
While the wot is cooking, toast the injera bread over an open flame or in a dry pan until crispy and slightly charred.
To serve, place a piece of injera bread in the center of a plate and spoon the wot over the bread.
Repeat with the remaining bread and wot, and serve immediately.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro or parsley, if desired.
Note: Traditionally, this dish is served with a side of salad or pickled vegetables, but it can also be served as a main course with a side of rice or roasted vegetables.
